name: Montejurra por el Camino Francés alternativo – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Dicastillo offers an extensive network of routes within the Tierra Estella-Lizarraldea region of Navarra, Spain. The terrain transitions from emblematic mountain ranges like Urbasa and Montejurra to flatter plains, providing varied riding experiences. Cyclists can navigate through lush forests, vineyards, and rolling hills, often traversing historical paths such as sections of the Camino de Santiago. This diverse topography supports a wide range of mountain biking, from technical climbs to relaxed rides through valleys. Best mountain bike trails around Dicastillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Montejurra por el Camino Francés alternativo – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT), a difficult 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Morentin-Dicastillo por el río Ega – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT), a moderate 14.4 miles (23.1 km) path. This route follows the Ega river, offering a mix of riverside paths and varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Multitrampas – Camping Iratxe loop from Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Irache, a 12.8 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes near historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Dicastillo is defined by diverse mountain ranges, fertile plains, and historical routes, with options for different ability levels. The region's extensive network includes singletracks and paths suitable for both mountain biking and gravel riding. The routes in Dicastillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Dicastillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Dicastillo?
A: There are over 150 mountain bike trails around Dicastillo, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes 17 easy routes, 103 moderate routes, and 35 difficult routes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Dicastillo?
A: The terrain around Dicastillo is incredibly diverse, transitioning from emblematic mountain ranges like Urbasa and Montejurra to flatter plains. You can expect to ride through lush forests, vineyards, and rolling hills. Many trails also traverse historical paths, including sections of the Camino de Santiago and ancient drovers' routes, providing a mix of challenging climbs, technical descents, and more relaxed rides through valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Dicastillo?
A: Yes, Dicastillo offers trails suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families. There are 17 easy routes available, and the Tierra Estella-Lizarraldea BTT Space, which includes Dicastillo, is designed to accommodate riders from families to experienced enthusiasts. These trails are well-marked and provide a great introduction to mountain biking in the region.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Dicastillo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Dicastillo are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10522738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Multitrampas – Camping Iratxe loop from Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Irache&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 12.8-mile (20.7 km) loop that takes you through varied landscapes near historical sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10522966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John the Baptist loop from Morentin&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 20.6-mile (33.2 km) route.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking near Dicastillo?
A: The region offers several interesting landmarks and natural features. You can encounter historical sites along the Camino de Santiago, or visit the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/382711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which dispenses both water and local wine. The medieval city of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby and can be explored by bicycle, offering cultural attractions and accommodation options.

Q: Are there any facilities or services for mountain bikers in the Dicastillo area?
A: Yes, the Tierra Estella-Lizarraldea BTT Space, encompassing Dicastillo, is well-equipped for cyclists. It features seven BTT reception centers that provide essential services such as small workshops, repair kits, bicycle rental, bike washing facilities, and comprehensive information about the MTB area and its routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Dicastillo?
A: The diverse topography of the region, with its mountains and plains, means that mountain biking can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding through forests, vineyards, and rolling hills, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region is known for its extensive network, including sections of historically significant paths. You can ride parts of the Camino de Santiago, ancient drovers' routes (Cañadas Reales), and the Navarre-Basque Railroad Greenway (Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro), which are suitable for longer rides and offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Dicastillo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Dicastillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Over 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, well-marked routes, and the mix of challenging and relaxed riding experiences.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 35 difficult routes available. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988526765/montejurra-por-el-camino-frances-alternativo-variante-circular-corta-a-la-integral-a-montejurra-mb7-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra por el Camino Francés alternativo – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT)&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 18.9-mile (30.4 km) trail that features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with viewpoints or scenic vistas?
A: Absolutely. The diverse topography of Dicastillo, ranging from mountain ranges to rolling hills, provides numerous opportunities for scenic views. Trails often pass through lush forests and vineyards, offering picturesque scenery. The region's natural beauty ensures an ample repertoire of routes with stunning vistas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that follow rivers or water features?
A: Yes, some trails incorporate water features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988518248/morentin-dicastillo-por-el-rio-ega-variante-circular-corta-a-la-integral-a-montejurra-mb7-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morentin-Dicastillo por el río Ega – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.4-mile (23.1 km) path that follows the Ega river, offering a mix of riverside paths and varied landscapes.
tips: Coinciding with the alternative French Way, this stretch of gravel track is one of the most picturesque in the area. With a surface in perfect condition, the route runs through the places known as "El Chorro" and "Los Enemigos" among wonderful planted fields that vary the landscape depending on the time of year you visit.
Vertex located next to the antennae. A perfect place for a sandwich, especially if you continue 100 meters towards "Los Cañones", to the meadow below.
Be careful with the final descent where you have to choose the right route to save the existing steps.

In the first part we find the steepest and twisty areas. Once we leave the forest area the path loses difficulty but it is advisable not to lower our guard due to the existence of stones on the edge of the path that we must avoid. the final part to Arellano runs along an old road very fast but with some area of stones to take into account.
Short and fun singletrack but with a lot of history within the MTB Navarro. Different races of both the Open DN and the CR Cup have passed both up and down.
